description: Official Lados workflow control primitives for triggers, branching, parallel flow, delay, and logging.
guardrails: Workflow controls must not approve, certify, or decide commercial entitlement.
Event publishing must preserve source resource and actor metadata when available.

verification.manifest: verified — S9.1 fix: the skeleton declared "workflow.control.loop" and "workflow.event.publish" capabilities with no corresponding nodes; added lados.workflow.loop and lados.workflow.publish_event to close both gaps. "workflow.trigger.event" remains capability-only by design — event-triggered workflow starts are handled by EventBusService subscription dispatch (see event-bus.service.ts), not by a runnable node; a placeholder node would have no executable behavior of its own. Phase 22 S22.4 (2026-07-06): added "workflow.control.switch" + lados.workflow.switch (true multi-way routing, 5 fixed case slots + default — see switch.ts header for why fixed rather than dynamic) and extended lados.workflow.condition's expression grammar (named fields + AND/OR combinator, backward compatible).
verification.runtime: implemented — 10 of 11 capabilities have a backing node (see manifest note above for the one intentional exception, workflow.trigger.event)
